premise: I'd given my sister $90,000 over fifteen years for home nurses and remodels so our mother could age in place, while my sister never visited, but when Mom passed, she produced a ‘new’ will — signed while Mom was on heavy sedatives — leaving me nothing and handing her the $380,000 house. I found a hidden video diary where Mom begged me to stop the coercion, sued for undue influence and caregiver reimbursement, won $150,000 plus the house, bought it back at the forced auction for $85,000, and converted it into an assisted-living home for seniors whose families had abandoned them — naming the wing after my sister.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Sister Forged A Will, Stole Mom's House, And Left Me Nothing. So I Named A Wi
